reasoning: `new_container_ensures` never constrains `can_have_children`. However, this may be intentionally left to the caller's policy — a new container's ability to have children could legitimately be either true or false depending on the use case.

`new_container_ensures` never constrains `owned_threads` of either the parent or the new container. A thread appearing in both parent and child simultaneously violates unique ownership, and the spec doesn't prevent this overlap during container creation.

No spec relates `root_process` to `owned_procs`. The ensures merely restates a precondition, but the real finding is that the preconditions are simultaneously satisfiable — `root_process == Some(p)` and `owned_procs@.contains(p)` have no required relationship enforced anywhere.

`new_container_ensures` never constrains `owned_cpus` of the new container. The ghost `Set<usize>` inside `ArraySet` can contain indices ≥ NUM_CPUS, violating the intended bounds of the fixed-size array backing.
